skill.name: seo
skill.description: Guides technical and on-page SEO improvements using Lighthouse SEO checks, Google Search guidance, metadata, sitemaps, and structured data patterns.
skill.summary: Audit and improve technical SEO, metadata, structured data, crawlability, and mobile search readiness.
skill.author: addyosmani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples, SEO reference URLs, and relative documentation links rather than executable code. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, credential access, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are documentation snippets for robots.txt, HTML, XML, JSON-LD, and CSS, and there is no executable script or command invocation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review confirmed these locations are Markdown fences and static HTML or configuration examples. No shell commands, Ruby execution, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Example and Reference URLs Are Non-Executable
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to example.com, schema.org, Google Search Central, and validation tools. They are instructional references, not automatic network requests or exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed URLs appear inside examples or reference lists. The skill does not include code that fetches, posts, or transmits local data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misclassified as Filesystem Traversal
low_findings.description: The relative paths link to neighboring skill documentation for Core Web Vitals and web quality auditing. They are Markdown references and do not read, write, or traverse the filesystem programmatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only traversal-like strings are relative Markdown links. There is no file API, shell command, or instruction to access arbitrary paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns on ordinary SEO text and HTML examples. Review found no cryptographic code, hashing function, encryption library, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are front matter, an HSTS header example, a meta description example, and checklist text. None implements or recommends weak cryptography.

content.value_statement: Technical SEO issues can block crawling, indexing, rich results, and mobile visibility. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code review pages and suggest concrete SEO fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews robots.txt, meta robots tags, canonical URLs, and sitemap structure.
Suggests title tags, meta descriptions, heading hierarchy, and image SEO improvements.
Provides JSON-LD patterns for Organization, Article, Product, FAQ, and Breadcrumb data.
Checks mobile SEO basics such as viewport settings, tap targets, and readable font sizes.
Creates prioritized SEO audit checklists for critical, high priority, medium priority, and ongoing tasks.
limitations: Does not measure live rankings, backlink authority, or search traffic by itself.
Requires page content, URLs, or audit results from the user for site-specific recommendations.
Does not replace Google Search Console, Lighthouse, or structured data validation tools.
Cannot guarantee ranking improvements because search engines use many external signals.

use_cases.title: Fix Launch Blocking SEO Issues
use_cases.description: Review a new site for crawlability, metadata, canonical tags, sitemap coverage, and mobile readiness before release.

use_cases.title: Improve Product Page Search Visibility
use_cases.description: Create stronger titles, descriptions, image alt text, internal links, and Product structured data guidance.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Editorial SEO Checklist
use_cases.description: Turn article content into a practical checklist for headings, metadata, schema, links, and indexability.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page for basic SEO problems. Focus on the title, meta description, headings, canonical URL, image alt text, and indexability.

prompt_templates.title: Technical SEO Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized technical SEO checklist for this site. Include robots.txt, sitemap, canonical URLs, HTTPS, mobile readiness, and structured data validation.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Data Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend structured data for these pages. Explain which schema types fit each page and list validation steps without adding unsupported claims.

prompt_templates.title: Full SEO Remediation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these Lighthouse, Search Console, and page examples. Group issues by impact, explain likely search effects, and produce an implementation plan for developers and content owners.

output_examples.input: A product page has a generic title, no meta description, missing alt text, and no product schema.
output: Priority: high. Add a unique product title that includes the main query and brand context.
Priority: high. Write a specific meta description that matches the page offer and avoids unsupported claims.
Priority: medium. Add descriptive alt text for product images and validate Product structured data before release.

output_examples.input: A blog article is indexed but has weak search snippets and unclear heading structure.
output: Use one clear H1 that matches the article topic and organize supporting sections with H2 and H3 headings.
Rewrite the meta description to summarize the article value in plain language.
Consider Article structured data if the page includes accurate author, publisher, and date information.

output_examples.input: A multilingual site needs international SEO guidance.
output: Add language declarations for each page and confirm every localized version has a matching alternate link.
Use a default alternate only when it points to the best fallback page.
Check that canonical URLs do not conflict with language alternates.

best_practices: Validate recommendations with Lighthouse, Google Search Console, and structured data testing tools when possible.
Base titles, descriptions, and schema fields on visible page content and accurate business facts.
Prioritize crawlability, indexability, canonical accuracy, and mobile usability before fine tuning snippets.
anti_patterns: Do not add structured data that describes content or offers missing from the page.
Do not block important assets or pages in robots.txt without a clear indexing reason.
Do not create duplicate titles, generic descriptions, or keyword-stuffed headings across many pages.

faq.question: Can this skill perform a live SEO crawl?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance from supplied pages, audit results, or code. Use a crawler or Search Console for live site data.

faq.question: Does this skill write structured data?
faq.answer: It recommends structured data patterns and validation steps. The final fields must match verified page content.

faq.question: Can it guarantee higher rankings?
faq.answer: No. It can improve technical and on-page signals, but rankings also depend on content quality, authority, competition, and search behavior.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide page URLs, HTML snippets, Lighthouse results, Search Console issues, sitemap examples, or current metadata.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-developers?
faq.answer: Yes. Content and marketing teams can use it for metadata, headings, internal links, and editorial SEO checklists.

faq.question: Does it cover Core Web Vitals?
faq.answer: It references page experience as part of SEO, but detailed performance analysis belongs in a Core Web Vitals workflow.
